timing_boundary: The first public commit is 6 minutes 20 seconds after the arXiv v1 submission timestamp. The audited next-day commit corrects the arXiv identifier; it is official current-release evidence, not proven byte-identical paper-time code.

paper_artifact_correspondence.paper_claim: Part III says the manuscript artifact contains benchmarks/source-benchmark.mjs, benchmarks/latest.json, benchmarks/publication-latest.json, artifacts/MANIFEST.md, artifacts/REPRODUCE.md, and main.pdf.
paper_artifact_correspondence.consequence: The public archive cannot replay or audit the paper's 96-seed, 384-variant benchmark, metric denominators, policy-degradation cases, Azure drills, red-team casebook outputs, or reported perfect scores. It contains the manuscript source, open-core library, examples, and five unit tests.

execution.tests: 5/5 Vitest unit tests passed; open-boundary scan passed for 13 files; npm package dry-run produced 15 files (16.0 kB packed, 55.0 kB unpacked).
execution.test_scope: The tests cover one custom adapter/action/receipt, approval action-id and semantic-key mismatch, telemetry/in-toto projection, one two-action parser decomposition, and source-boundary string checks. They do not execute the paper benchmark or production parsers, policy routing, signer, approval workflow, action lease, evidence graph, outcome closure, cloud drill, or managed runtime.

positive_witnesses: Object-key ordering is stable under the custom canonical serializer.
CAVA-IR excludes runtime family/adapter from semantic_key, so the tested equivalent input converges across shell and MCP labels.
The bundled tests reject changed action IDs and changed honest semantic keys.
negative_witnesses: The canonical-action fingerprint changes when systems_touched order changes and when runtime/adapter changes.
CAVA-IR includes operation.raw, so two otherwise identical wrapper surfaces do not converge.
createCanonicalAction({}) fills unknown/default values and validateCanonicalAction accepts the result as valid.
A receipt with a caller-supplied canonical_fingerprint inconsistent with its embedded canonical_action verifies as valid because verification checks only the outer receipt hash.
An approval binding with binding_hash removed verifies as valid.
A caller-supplied IR carrying an old semantic_key but changed fields verifies against the old approval because normalizeIr trusts any object with semantic_key.
verifyCavaExecution({}) returns execution_verified with no checks or observation evidence.
Approval verification has no expected policy/snapshot/adapter/time-window argument, so the retained stale example remains valid.
A throwing parser pack falls back to a valid unknown/non-reversible action while retaining only a warning.
The reference SQL parser classifies SELECT text containing DELETE in a comment as database_mutation.

open_core_omissions: Production parser packs and parser quality evidence
Semantic Pattern Layer implementation and scored pattern cases
PCAA policy routing and authority workflow
Bounded Action Firewall leases, expiry, replay resistance, and consumption
Signatures, KMS/HSM, verifiable credentials, and ledger anchoring
Managed runtime adapters/connectors and evidence graph
Raw benchmark cases, per-case results, aggregation code, and deployment evidence
claim_ceiling: The audited release supports a small deterministic JavaScript skeleton, local hash/receipt construction, interface examples, and bounded mutation findings at one post-v1 commit. It does not reproduce the paper benchmark or establish semantic correctness, robust runtime equivalence, approval freshness/authority, observed execution, policy correctness, production governance, professional validity, safety, or readiness.
